item.title: OpenAI teams up with Infosys to bring AI tools to more businesses
item.dc:creator: Jagmeet Singh
item.description: Infosys said the integration will be used to help its clients modernize software development, automate workflows and deploy AI systems, initially focusing software engineering, legacy modernization, and DevOps.

item.title: UK government says 100 countries have spyware that can hack people’s phones
item.dc:creator: Zack Whittaker
item.description: The U.K.'s cybersecurity chief warned that U.K. businesses and critical infrastructure are underestimating the threat from spyware attacks and other cyberthreats, with more governments having access to the powerful surveillance technology than ever.

item.title: X makes it more expensive to post links through its API
item.dc:creator: Ivan Mehta
item.description: Posting links on X through its API is now 1,900% more costly

item.title: Amazon Music partners with Bandsintown for concert listings
item.dc:creator: Ivan Mehta
item.description: Users will be able to see live events of an artist on their Amazon Music profile

item.title: AI is spitting out more potential drugs than ever. This start-up wants to figure out which ones matter.
item.dc:creator: Tim Fernholz
item.description: 10x Science has raised a $4.8 million seed round to help pharmaceutical researchers understand complex molecules.

item.title: Snap Map’s new ‘Place Loyalty’ badges will show the spots you visit most often
item.dc:creator: Aisha Malik
item.description: If you’re in the top 25% of users who visit a certain place, you’ll see your ranking on the Snap Map and can choose to share it with others.

item.title: The most interesting startups showcased at Google Cloud Next 2026
item.dc:creator: Julie Bort
item.description: Google wants AI startups on its cloud and has showcased a long list of them at its annual conference.

item.title: Duolingo is now giving free users access to advanced learning content
item.dc:creator: Lauren Forristal
item.description: Duolingo is opening up its advanced language learning content offering to free users, allowing them to acquire skills that were previously reserved to paid subscribers.

item.title: Google Maps is about to get a big dose of AI
item.dc:creator: Lucas Ropek
item.description: Generative AI is being infused into Google's popular feature within Maps.

item.title: Exclusive: Google deepens Thinking Machines Lab ties with new multi-billion-dollar deal
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Bellan
item.description: Mira Murati's Thinking Machines Lab has signed a multi-billion-dollar deal with Google Cloud for AI infrastructure powered by Nvidia's latest GB300 chips, TechCrunch has exclusively learned.

item.title: Redwood Materials lays off 10% in restructuring to chase energy storage business
item.dc:creator: Sean O'Kane
item.description: The company is restructuring some teams to accommodate a booming energy storage business, according to emails viewed by TechCrunch.

item.title: Meta will record employees’ keystrokes and use it to train its AI models
item.dc:creator: Lucas Ropek
item.description: Meta says that it has a new internal tool that is converting mouse movements and button clicks into data that can train its AI models.

item.title: Unauthorized group has gained access to Anthropic’s exclusive cyber tool Mythos, report claims
item.dc:creator: Lucas Ropek
item.description: Anthropic told TechCrunch it is investigating the claims, but maintains that there is no evidence that its systems have been impacted.

item.title: SpaceX is working with Cursor and has an option to buy the startup for $60B
item.dc:creator: Tim Fernholz
item.description: The move could shore up weaknesses at each company, but it also reveals them. Neither Cursor nor xAI has proprietary models that can match the leading offerings from Anthropic and OpenAI — the same companies now competing directly with Cursor for the developer market.

item.title: Tim Cook is stepping down as CEO of Apple: Here’s a look at his 15-year legacy, from new products and services to China expansion
item.dc:creator: Aisha Malik
item.description: Cook, who joined Apple in 1998, succeeded Steve Jobs as CEO in 2011 and went on to transform Apple into a $4 trillion powerhouse.

item.title: Apple’s John Ternus will run one of the world’s most powerful companies; the job is a minefield
item.dc:creator: Connie Loizos
item.description: Apple's top job comes with almost unrivaled power and money, but it comes with plenty of baggage, too.

item.title: AI research lab NeoCognition lands $40M seed to build agents that learn like humans
item.dc:creator: Marina Temkin
item.description: Founded by an OSU researcher, the startup is developing AI agents that can become experts in any domain.

item.title: Apple’s Cal AI crackdown signals it’s still policing the App Store
item.dc:creator: Sarah Perez
item.description: Apple tells TechCrunch Cal AI was removed not for web payments alone, but for deceptive billing, manipulative tactics, and App Store rule violations.

item.title: SusHi Tech Tokyo isn’t a conference — it’s a deal room with 60,000 people
item.dc:creator: Cindy Zackney
item.description: When 60,000 attendees descend on Tokyo Big Sight April 27–29, the headline numbers are hard to ignore: 750 startup exhibitors, 151 sessions, city leaders from 49 countries. But the stat that tells you what kind of event this actually is? It's 10,000 facilitated business meetings — brokered, booked, and tracked before most attendees even land.

item.title: ChatGPT’s new Images 2.0 model is surprisingly good at generating text
item.dc:creator: Amanda Silberling
item.description: ChatGPT Images 2.0, the newest image-generation model from OpenAI, shows just how much AI capabilities have evolved over the last few years.
